text: And this is actually pretty important when we talk about progressive disclosure, because the main difference between all of these tools, say you wanted to put everything into your agents.md file or your cloud.md file.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Every time you create a new session, all of that is loaded into your context.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: The same applies into MCPs.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And we talked again about this, one of the disadvantages in MCPs.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Everything is loaded into your context, which means regardless, every time I open up Cloud Code or anyone opens a new session, that context window is already taken up because you've added all these instructions.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: That's the disadvantage.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: The way skills goes about this is this YAML front matter that I talked about.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: It only takes the name and description and loads that into the context window.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Only the words in your name and description are converted into tokens and used in your context window.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

speaker: SPEAKER_01

speaker: SPEAKER_01

speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Because I'm not sure if you have faced this situation, but if you have an agent and you want this agent to do the thing repeatedly, right, which is like search a folder, analyze, come back with a result or make multiple API requests.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: If it's making 50 requests because, you know, it pulls a list.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: of IDs, and then it has to make an API request for each.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: When you start to do these repetitive tasks, that's a good reason to use context fork because you don't kind of want all of that API request and the back and forth to go into your main agent.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: You just want the results, especially when you have this repetitive process.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: So that's another good reason to use context fork.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: When you find yourself having these small but repeated tasks, you should probably push all of that into isolated subagents with this field.

text: And just as a nuance, a common question people ask is if you have this context fork and it's pushed to a separate sub-agent, how does the main agent know what results to pick up?
speaker: SPEAKER_01
